title: Black pepper and amber reed diffuser
subtitle: A warm, spiced reed diffuser blending black pepper and amber fragrance oil in a DPG carrier
excerpt: Black pepper and amber fragrance oil sits in the warm-spiced oriental family: rich, resinous, and slightly sharp. In a reed diffuser it diffuses slowly, scenting a room continuously over 2 to 3 months. The carrier is dipropylene glycol, which is safe, low-odour, and slows the evaporation rate relative to alcohol-based diffusers.
sourceNotes: Reed diffuser formulation. Dipropylene glycol (DPG) as carrier: slow evaporation, very low odour, safe for room use. Fragrance oil at 25 to 30% of total volume: higher than candle load because there is no heat to amplify the throw. Rattan reeds: 5 to 8 for a 100 ml bottle. Flip reeds every 2 weeks to refresh the throw. Room coverage: small to medium room (up to approximately 20 sq m).

recipe.yieldDescription: ~100 ml diffuser (fills a standard 100 ml diffuser bottle)
recipe.batchNotes: Scale proportionally. Keep fragrance at 25 to 30% of DPG weight.
recipe.shelfLifeNotes: 6 months once the reeds are inserted and the bottle is open. Unopened, the filled bottle lasts 12 months. Fragrance fades gradually; replace reeds after 3 months if the throw weakens.

glossaryTerms.definition: A slow-evaporating, low-odour solvent used as the carrier in reed diffusers. DPG slows fragrance oil evaporation compared to alcohol-based carriers, extending the diffuser's life. It is non-toxic and widely used in home fragrance products.

The capillary action by which rattan or fibre reeds draw the diffuser liquid upward through their porous channels. The soaked top ends of the reeds evaporate fragrance into the surrounding air. Flipping the reeds renews the wet top ends and refreshes the scent throw.

content.text: A reed diffuser is one of the simplest fragrance projects. Dipropylene glycol
as the carrier slows evaporation and makes the fragrance last far longer than an alcohol-based diffuser.

draws the liquid up and releases it into the room. Flip the reeds upside down every 2 weeks to bring fresh liquid to the top and refresh the scent. Fragrance oil is flammable; keep the bottle away from open flames and direct heat.

items.prepNote: 25% fragrance load; higher than candles because no heat

content.text: Weigh DPG and fragrance oil into the beaker and stir to combine. Pour into the diffuser bottle. Insert the reeds. Allow 30 minutes for the reeds to absorb the liquid before placing in the room.
6 months open. Replace reeds after 3 months if the throw weakens. Top up with fresh diffuser liquid when the bottle is less than a quarter full.
